title: Syngonanthus arenarius
taxonomy: Syngonanthus arenarius is classified as a species of plant in the family Eriocaulaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as pipeworts. This family is part of the order Poales, which includes many other types of flowering plants such as grasses and sedges. The order Poales is part of the class Liliopsida, which is a group of flowering plants that have flowers with three petals and sepals. The class Liliopsida is part of the division Magnoliophyta, which includes all flowering plants. Syngonanthus arenarius is also known to be a member of the genus Syngonanthus, which is a group of flowering plants that are native to South America.
